message.group-title: Intensive Care and Critical Care Medicine
message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Importance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Critical care resources like ventilators, used to manage the current COVID-19 pandemic, are potentially inadequate. Established triage standards and guidelines may not contain the most appropriate severity assessment and outcome prediction models.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Develop a draft pandemic specific triage assessment score for the current COVID-19 pandemic. Design a website where initial Toward a COVID-19 Scores (TACS) can be quickly calculated and used to compare various treatment strategies. Create a TACS Registry where data and outcomes for suspected and confirmed COVID-19 patients can be recorded. Use the TACS Registry to develop an influenza epidemic specific database and score for use in future respiratory based epidemics.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design, Setting, Participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Retrospective analysis of 3,301 ICU admissions with respiratory failure admitted to 41 U.S. Intensive Care Units from 2015-19. Independent external validation on 1,175 similar ICU Admissions using identical entry criteria from Barnes Jewish Hospital (BJH), Washington University from 2016-2019.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main Outcomes</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>TACS was created with 16 readily available predictive variables for risk assessment of hospital mortality 24 hours after ICU Admission and the need for prolonged assisted mechanical ventilation (PAMV) (> 96 hours) at 24- and 48-hours post ICU admission.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>TACS achieved an Area Under the Curve (AUC) for hospital mortality after 24 hours of 0.80 in the development dataset; 0.81 in the internal validation dataset. At a probability of 50% hospital mortality, positive predictive value (PPV) was 0.55, negative predictive value (NPV) 0.89; sensitivity 22%, specificity 97%.</jats:p>
                  <jats:p>For PAMV after 24 hours, the AUC was 0.84 in the development dataset, 0.81 in the validation dataset. For PAMV after 48 hours, the AUC was 0.82 in the development dataset, 0.78 in the validation dataset.</jats:p>
                  <jats:p>
                    In the external validation the AUC for TACS was 0.76 +/- 0.024. We launched a website that is scaled for mobile device use (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://covid19score.azurewebsites.net/">https://covid19score.azurewebsites.net/</jats:ext-link>
                    ) that provides open access to a user-friendly TACS Calculator for all predictions. We also designed a voluntary TACS Registry for collection of data and outcomes on ICU Admissions with COVID-19.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions and Relevance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Toward a COVID-19 score is a starting point for an epidemic specific triage assessment that could be used to evaluate various approaches to treatment. The TACS Registry provides the ability to establish a respiratory specific outcomes database that can be used to create a triage approach for future such pandemics.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Key Points</jats:title>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Question</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Can a rapid epidemic specific risk assessment severity score and data and outcome repository be constructed in the midst of a pandemic.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Using development and validation datasets with ICU admissions similar to those developing COVID-19, developed an initial Toward a COVID-19 Score that could be used to compare various treatment approaches. Also launched an online facilitated data collection and outcome assessment registry for collection of a pandemic specific database so a new triage score could be created for use in the next pandemic.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Meaning</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>In the midst of a pandemic rapid development of an epidemic specific triage score and a data registry for the creation of a new score for use in future pandemics appears feasible.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                </jats:sec>
title: TOWARD A COVID-19 SCORE-RISK ASSESSMENTS AND REGISTRY

reference.unstructured: Law NYSTF on L and the, New York State Department Health. Ventilator Allocation Guidelines. November 2015. https://www.health.ny.gov/regulations/taskforce/reportspublications/docs/ventilatorguidelines.pdf.

Lawrence E Ho, MD , Assistant Professor of Medicine, Pulmonary and Critical Care Medicine, University of Washington, Seattle. Contacted on March 23, 2020. John E. Arbo, MD , Associate Director of the Albert Einstein / Jacobi Medical Center Department of Emergency Medicine, New York, NY. Contacted March 24, 2020. David Kauffman, MD ., Medical Director, Intensive Care Unit, New York University, New York, NY. Contacted March 24, 2020. Chanu Rhee, MD , Assistant Professor of Medicine, Harvard School of Medicine, Brigham and Women’s Hospital, Boston, MA. Contacted March 24, 2020. Michael S. Neiderman, MD , Professor of Pulmonary and Critical Care Medicine, Weill Cornell Medicine, New York, NY. Contacted March 24, 2020. Antonio Artigas, MD , Professor of Medicine and Critical Care Division Chief, Universidad Autonoma de Barcelona, Spain. Contacted March 25, 2020. Daniel De Backer , Brussels, Belgium. Contacted 3/25/2020. Douglas B. White MD , Vice Chair and Professor of Critical Care Medicine, University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine. Contacted March 30, 2020.
